description_long: This building features a grand rectangular structure with a prominent colonnade of large, evenly spaced columns running along its length and at both ends, reminiscent of ancient Greek or Roman temples. The roof is a steeply pitched gable covered in reddish-brown tiles, with a triangular pediment at each short end. The facade is adorned with a row of blue decorative elements just below the roofline, adding a touch of color to the otherwise beige stone exterior. The main entrance is centrally located on the short side, framed by a set of columns and a wide staircase leading up to it. The windows are minimal, with only a few small openings visible above the entrance. The overall impression is one of solidity, grandeur, and classical symmetry, suitable for a prestigious institution such as a bank.
description: A grand, classical stone building with a colonnade, red-tiled gable roof, and blue decorative accents, evoking the style of an ancient temple. The main entrance is framed by columns and a wide staircase.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main entrance with a wide staircase, six large columns, and a triangular pediment above, flanked by blue decorative elements.
description_view_1: The side view reveals the full length of the colonnade with many evenly spaced columns and the long, red-tiled roof stretching above.
description_view_2: The rear view mirrors the front, with a similar triangular pediment and column arrangement but no visible entrance.
description_view_3: The opposite side view again displays the extensive colonnade and the length of the building, emphasizing its symmetry.
attribute_entrance_design: The main entrance features a wide staircase leading to a set of double doors, framed by six large stone columns and topped with a triangular pediment; the doors are likely heavy and made of metal or wood, with minimal decorative elements.
